texts.title: Aeneid — opening line
texts.transliteration: Arma virumque canō, Trōiae quī prīmus ab ōrīs
texts.translation: I sing of arms and the man, who first from the shores of Troy…
texts.notes: Standard hook line for Classical Latin phonology renders; manuscript tradition secure.

texts.title: De Bello Gallico — opening line
texts.transliteration: Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres
texts.translation: Gaul as a whole consists of three parts
texts.notes: Primary training block for Caesar History figure voice and period prose.

texts.title: Meditations — note on source language
texts.source: Marcus Aurelius wrote in Greek; Latin Stoic vocabulary still relevant for renders
texts.notes: Cross-language: Greek primary, Latin philosophical loan vocabulary.
